title: Container Creation, Process Lifecycle, CLI State, and Cleanup · Deployment Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective deterministic container lifecycle runbook; isolate the first causal fault in container creation, process lifecycle, cli state, and cleanup and preserve a regression check before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in deterministic container lifecycle runbook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Distinguish create, run, start, attach, exec, stop, kill, restart, pause, wait, remove, and auto-remove state transitions.
Apply names, environment, working directory, user, entrypoint, command, labels, hosts, and platform overrides with correct precedence.
Choose foreground, detached, interactive, TTY, attach, and log behavior while preserving standard streams and exit evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Create Run Start Boundary
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug operator inherits 5 similarly named containers: one created, one running, one exited with code 137, and one carrying the only writable-layer report. Inspect identity and state, preserve evidence, then perform label-scoped cleanup. steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Cleanup Scope Labels
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-cleanup-scope-labels-9q3sfg". Tailor its container-lifecycle.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Cleanup Scope Labels.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Cleanup Scope Labels into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Cleanup Scope Labels, keep Docker Cleanup Scope Labels connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-cleanup-scope-labels-9q3sfg
  - competency: docker-cleanup-scope-labels
    artifact: container-lifecycle.yaml
    decision: cleanup scope labels
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: Docker Desktop VM with synchronized source files
    fault-injection: send termination during an in-flight durable operation
    retained-proof: timestamped lifecycle transition and bounded cleanup result
    case-key: 9q3sfg
    change: |
      create: immutable-configured-container
      start: same-container-identity
      inspect: state-before-mutation
      cleanup: label-scoped-and-previewed
    verify: docker container inspect training-web --format json
    failure: Reject name-only cleanup, unbounded prune, or a claim that stop and remove preserve writable-layer state.
    repair: Use explicit IDs or labels, inspect before mutation, capture exit state, and remove only the scoped disposable resource.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
